classification.proof: Profile data is classified as displayName, locale, and timezone; notification preferences are classified by channel, category, and enabled flag; notification dispatch records are classified by channel, category, status, and subject. retention.proof: Profile, notification_preferences, and notification_log are tenant/user-scoped Postgres records and participate in the platform retention model for persisted tenant data. The capability itself does not create an ungoverned duplicate store; profile and preference proofs validate source rows and RLS while retention proof governs persisted records.

backup.proof: Profile and preference state is Postgres-backed, so database backup covers user_profiles, notification_preferences, and notification_log rows. Runtime proofs validate those rows are the source of truth rather than a generated report or cache.

restore.proof: Restore follows the database substrate: restored user_profiles, notification_preferences, and notification_log rows are read through the same repositories and routes. PITR/restore proofs cover the database, while profile/preference proofs validate read-back semantics from live Postgres.

export.proof: Tenant export includes governed tenant history and identity/config artefacts; profile and preference mutations are captured as audited/history-visible records and persisted in tenant-scoped Postgres tables. The data-portability proof verifies encrypted archive generation and audit/history export.

legal hold.proof: Legal hold applies at the persisted tenant-record layer: profile and preference changes are audited before mutation, and held durable source rows are governed by the platform legal-hold and retention proofs. The self-service usecases do not delete profile/preference records or bypass legal-hold-controlled source rows.

DSR.proof: DSR/data-portability coverage is represented by tenant export plus the direct self-service read contracts: users can read their own profile and preferences, updates are audited, and export includes the resulting safe history projection. Secret-bearing notification payload fields are rejected before logging or delivery.

lineage.proof: Lineage is explicit from authenticated session to storage and audit: routes derive organisationId and userId from tenant context/session, usecases pass those values to repositories, profile updates emit ProfileUpdated, preference updates emit NotificationPreferencesChanged, and dispatch produces notification_log status rows.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Policy lifecycle is read current resource policy -> audit -> set replacement policy; request evaluation is UMA-first when a token exists, static fallback on unavailable/unregistered resource, policy-denied fail-closed, and step-up on insufficient auth level. Evidence: authorize-resource and resource-policies unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is route guard resource + umaScope + requiredPermission, optional ResourcePolicy records, UMA decision granted|policy_denied|insufficient_auth_level|keycloak_unavailable|resource_not_registered, and static permission fallback. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/authorize-resource.ts and tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Routes declare explicit requiredPermission, resource, and umaScope; resource policy writes are behind tenant auth-settings permission and use audit-before-adapter mutation. Evidence: routes.ts, authorize-resource tests, and resource-policies tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is /api/auth/settings/resource-policies plus per-route resource/umaScope guard metadata consumed by the pipeline and GraphQL authorizer. Evidence: routes.ts and apps/platform-api/tests/unit/resource-policies.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown or unregistered UMA resources degrade only to the static permission backstop; absent static permission denies; injected resource policy may allow without static permission; wildcard resource policy names are accepted. Evidence: authorize-resource and resource-policies tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Policy denied maps to 403, insufficient_auth_level maps to stepUpRequired/401, missing token when one is expected maps to authenticationRequired/401, and static fallback without permission maps to 403. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/authorize-resource.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Resource policy mutation emits audit before adapter set; audit failure prevents adapter mutation. Authorization checks are read-only and logged/returned rather than audited per request. Evidence: resource-policies unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready for the local policy seam; Keycloak unavailable and resource_not_registered are explicit degraded/non-decisive states with static fallback rather than fake allow. Evidence: authorize-resource tests and API pipeline UMA tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/authorize-resource.test.ts and resource-policies.test.ts. Runtime proof: proof:entitlement-policy-chain covers permission-before-entitlement policy ordering. Proof level: 3 state/policy-chain validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose resource-policy read/write surfaces only to permitted auth-settings actors, show resource/scope/permission vocabulary, present step-up/forbidden/unavailable states distinctly, and never imply UMA grants when static fallback is the actual decision source.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Login/session lifecycle is explicit: /auth/login creates a PKCE state and pre-auth nonce, /auth/callback validates state/code/nonce and resolves identity to a Redis session, /api/session reads fixture or real session cookies, and logout destroys/clears host-only plus domain-scoped cookies. Evidence: auth-routes, auth-usecase, session-fixture, and cookie-security tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: Session state is SessionRecord with sessionId, userId, tenantId, organisationId, roles, permissions, displayName, expiry, and Redis-backed cookie id; no-membership and system-admin states have empty tenant context by design. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/usecases/auth.ts and auth-usecase tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: The capability establishes the actor rather than requiring a permission; resolved roles are converted to explicit permission bundles, with tenant roles sourced from membership and system-admin sourced from realm roles without tenant membership. Evidence: auth-usecase and domain-identity tests.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /auth/login, GET /auth/callback, POST/GET /auth/logout, GET /api/session, and GET /internal/auth/forward. Provider selection is surfaced through GET /api/auth/providers. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Login validates/sanitises returnTo, emits PKCE challenge parameters, binds callback to the pre-auth cookie, rejects missing/unknown state/code and Keycloak error callbacks, and only accepts stored session cookies. Evidence: apps/platform-api/tests/substrate/auth-routes.test.ts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Unauthenticated /api/session returns 401 UNAUTHENTICATED; callback failures redirect to /login?authError=signin_failed; logout is idempotent 204; Redis/session lookup failure falls through to unauthenticated without leaking provider detail. Evidence: routes and auth-routes tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Login/session creation is represented by auth events and session store state; logout removes session state. Mutating auth-settings surfaces have separate audit-first records, while read-only session lookup has no mutation audit. Evidence: capability auditAction and auth-settings audit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness depends on Keycloak/Redis/session configuration, with fixture mode explicit for deterministic E2E and auth credential readiness used for tenant realm settings. Evidence: keycloak mapper readiness, auth-routes substrate tests, and proof:auth-settings.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Substrate/unit proofs: auth-routes, auth-usecase, session-fixture, cookie-security, token-crypto, and session-config tests. Runtime proof: proof:auth-settings validates live Keycloak auth settings round trips adjacent to the session auth substrate. Proof level: 4 for live auth provider substrate plus 3 for session state behavior.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must model login as a browser redirect handoff, expose provider choices from /api/auth/providers, render authenticated/unauthenticated/session-loading states from /api/session, treat callback errors as login-page failures, support logout navigation, and never expose Keycloak secrets or raw tokens.

semanticCompleteness.auditModel: MFA, lockout, and session changes emit AuthSettingsMfaChanged, AuthSettingsLockoutChanged, and AuthSettingsSessionChanged with safe policy metadata before adapter mutation; audit failure prevents mutation. Evidence: auth-settings-audit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is credential-derived and provider-backed: configured/ok only when tenant realm credential can mutate/read policies, with invalid/forbidden/unreachable classified rather than hidden. semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proof: proof:auth-settings validates live Keycloak readiness, MFA read/write/read-back/restore, and session policy write/read-back/restore. Unit proofs validate audit-first ordering and schemas. Proof level: 4 live provider validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose MFA, lockout, and session policy forms/tabs, read and write permission gates, validation for enum/numeric bounds, no-credential/invalid/forbidden/unreachable readiness states, save success/failure, and no display of tenant credentials.

semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is PLATFORM_CONFIG_DEFINITIONS plus tenant_settings storageKey values, source default|tenant_override, valueType string|enum|boolean|json, allowedValues, tenantOverridable, per-definition permissions, lifecycle, and feature flag compatibility transforms. semanticCompleteness.permissions: GET /api/org/config requires tenant.config.read and additionally filters definitions by requiredPermissionRead; PATCH/DELETE require tenant.config.write and usecase-enforce each definition's requiredPermissionWrite. semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET /api/org/config?category=, PATCH /api/org/config/:key with {value}, and DELETE /api/org/config/:key; response items include serialised definition, value, and source. Evidence: routes.ts and contracts-admin config DTOs.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Unknown keys, non-overridable definitions, missing value, enum values outside allowedValues, type mismatches, category filters, and insufficient per-definition permissions are explicit. Evidence: platform-config tests and contracts-admin validateConfigValue.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Routes map invalid_body to 400, not_found to 404, not_overridable to 422, forbidden to 403, no tenant to 400, and successful writes/deletes to 204; webhook fan-out failure is best-effort and does not roll back config mutation. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Set emits the definition auditAction, clear emits ConfigValueCleared, both before tenant_settings mutation with key/value metadata; audit failure prevents DB writes. Evidence: platform-config tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is invariant-ready once tenant_settings/Postgres is available; definition registry is pure and serialisable, and history/audit projection is covered by the live history proof. Evidence: registry.ts and proof:history.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/platform-config.test.ts and contracts-admin config validation tests. Adjacent runtime proof: proof:history validates live audit/history projection for config changes. Proof level: 3 for state/validation plus 4 for live history projection.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must generate config controls from definition DTOs, render source default vs tenant_override, category filters, allowed enum values, boolean/json/string editors, per-definition permission/read-only states, validation errors, clear override action, and history/audit links.

semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Theme read failures fall back to DEFAULT_THEME rather than blocking the SPA; registry branding mutations use config error mapping for validation, forbidden, unknown, and not-overridable states. Evidence: routes.ts and platform-config tests.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: GET /api/theme is read-only; branding config mutations emit ConfigValueChanged or ConfigValueCleared before tenant_settings writes. Evidence: platform-config usecase/tests.

semanticCompleteness.permissions: Domain operations are tenant-scoped by tenant.domains.read/write; auth-client mutation is performed through the injected AuthClientDomainPort after ownership is verified. Evidence: capability record and apps/platform-api/src/usecases/tenant-domain-lifecycle.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: API contract surface is /api/org/domains*, including challenge creation, verification, activation/deactivation, routing probe, canonical set/unset, and list/readiness projection. Evidence: capability record, docs/api/openapi.json, and domain route/runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Domain validation rejects invalid names and IP literals; DNS verification requires the expected _aldous-verify TXT token; canonical is refused until verified, active, and locally routed; cross-tenant enabled-domain claims are rejected before token issuance. semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed outcomes include invalid_domain, domain_already_claimed, not_found, expired, already_verified, dns_not_found, dns_mismatch, not_verified, auth_client_inactive, routing_not_proven, not_active, and ok. semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness distinguishes no_domains, pending_dns, verified, inactive auth-client, local routing proven, public DNS/TLS deferred, and canonical redirect cutover; local proofs explicitly do not claim public DNS/TLS readiness. semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:tenant-domains, proof:tenant-domains-routing, proof:tenant-domain-canonical, proof:tenant-domain-claim-lifecycle, proof:tenant-custom-domain-resolution, and proof:tenant-custom-domain-auth-origin. semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose domain list/readiness, challenge creation with TXT record, verify/retry states, activation/deactivation, local routing status, canonical set/unset, conflict and invalid-domain errors, deferred public TLS/DNS labels, permission-denied states, and no token leakage after creation.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Secret lifecycle is write-only create/rotate/update, validate-before-store where applicable, store encrypted/hashed or provider-masked value, expose only hasSecret/readiness metadata, preserve existing secret when blank/absent, and clear/delete through owning resource lifecycle. Evidence: idp-management, credential-lifecycle, token-crypto, and auth-settings-audit tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes tenant admin credential clientId/clientSecret lifecycle metadata, IdP hasClientSecret and Keycloak-masked clientSecret, SMTP/webhook/API-key secret stores, token crypto encrypted or unenc test prefix state, and readiness classifications without raw secret output. Evidence: relevant secret usecases and proofs.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Secret writes are controlled by the owning surface permissions such as tenant.auth.settings.write, platform provider config permissions, webhook write permissions, and API key create/revoke scopes; reads return redacted metadata only. Evidence: route metadata and proof inventory.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts include IdP create/update clientSecret write-only fields, auth credential lifecycle rotation, email sender/webhook secret write routes, and API-key reveal-once create response; no contract supports later raw secret retrieval. Evidence: routes, usecases, and runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Secrets are validated before storage or provider mutation, unsafe URLs/config are rejected, invalid tenant credentials are classified before store, empty IdP clientSecret preserves existing value, and encryption key requirements are enforced for staging/production. Evidence: credential-lifecycle proof and token-crypto tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Typed states include no_credential, invalid_credential, forbidden_realm_operation, realm_unreachable, validation errors, not_found, and provider-specific readiness states; failures never echo secret values. Evidence: auth-settings usecase, sendAuthSettingsFailure, and secret proofs.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Audit records only safe metadata such as alias, changed field names, hasClientSecret, secretChanged, config key names, and readiness result; audit failure blocks mutation before secret-bearing provider/storage writes. Evidence: auth-settings-audit and idp-management tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness reports configured/missing/invalid/forbidden/unreachable/provider-unavailable without exposing secret material, and provider-backed secret stores require live substrate validation. Evidence: provider-secrets, auth-settings, and credential-lifecycle runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proofs: token-crypto, auth-settings-audit, idp-management. Runtime proofs: proof:auth-credential-lifecycle, proof:auth-idps, proof:api-keys, provider secret proofs. Proof level: 4 for live provider/storage-backed secret validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must render secret fields as write-only create/rotate controls, show configured/has-secret/readiness metadata, never prefill or reveal stored secrets, treat blank IdP secret as preserve existing, warn on destructive rotation/clear, and surface validation/provider errors without secret echo.

semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is Product(productId,name,description,isActive,createdAt), Plan(planId,productId,name,currency,billingPeriod,isActive,createdAt), and Price(priceId,planId,version,priceType,unitAmount,currency,billingPeriod,isActive,createdAt). semanticCompleteness.permissions: Operator catalog list/create routes require platform.billing.* with admin:billing UMA read/write; tenant catalog read requires tenant.billing.read and is tenant-scoped. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/server/routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contract surface is GET/POST /api/admin/billing/catalog/products, /plans, /prices, plus GET /api/org/billing/catalog; plan and price list routes accept productId/planId filters. Evidence: routes.ts and contracts-admin billing catalog schemas.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Create bodies are validated by CreateBillingProductRequestSchema, CreateBillingPlanRequestSchema, and CreateBillingPriceRequestSchema; invalid bodies return VALIDATION_ERROR before adapter calls. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Route errors are validation 400 for bad create bodies and permission denials before handlers; successful creates return 201 with created entity, lists return 200. Evidence: routes.ts.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Catalog mutations carry actorId into the usecase/adapter layer and are recorded as billing.catalog.changed per capability auditAction; route proof is not itself an audit proof. Evidence: capability record and billing-catalog usecase inputs.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is migration/catalog-seed backed: route registration proof validates the exposed surface and unit tests validate the in-process catalog behavior; provider billing readiness remains a separate capability. Evidence: proof:billing-catalog and billing-catalog tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Unit proof: apps/platform-api/tests/unit/billing-catalog.test.ts. Runtime route proof: proof:billing-catalog validates catalog route registration. semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose product, plan, and price tables, create forms, product/plan filters, tenant read-only catalog view, operator-only create actions, validation errors, permission-denied states, inactive item display, and currency/period/price typing.

semanticCompleteness.lifecycle: Billing lifecycle is ensure account/customer, sync plan/price, create subscription active, update/pause subscription, cancel subscription, fetch invoice, record usage event into provider, charge payment, and refund payment. Evidence: billing-provider and lago-billing-provider runtime proofs/tests.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model includes account/customer externalAccountId, subscriptionId/status active|paused|cancelled, planId, priceId, invoice status/open amounts/currency, payment charge outcome/idempotency key, and refund success state. Evidence: billing-provider ports and Lago adapter tests.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Operator/provider surfaces are gated through billing/provider control permissions; tenant-facing billing reads use tenant.billing.* semantics from capability contract and route metadata. Evidence: capability record and billing control/provider route proofs.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are BillingProviderPort, PaymentProviderPort, and SubscriptionPort operations for readiness, account, plan, subscription, invoice, usage, payment capture, and refund; Lago adapter maps the same port contract to HTTP/SDK calls. Evidence: apps/platform-api/src/ports/billing-provider.ts and Lago tests.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Provider adapters validate readiness before use, map SDK/HTTP responses into canonical DTOs, preserve idempotency keys for payment/usage, and keep amount/currency/status fields explicit. Evidence: billing-provider-runtime-proof and lago-billing-provider tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Readiness can be ready/unavailable via provider health; provider failures surface through adapter errors, missing invoices return null, and payment/refund outcomes are explicit rather than inferred from UI state. Evidence: billing-provider ports and runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Billing operations are represented by immutable ledger/provider records plus payment capture/refund records; operator actorId is part of account/plan/subscription inputs. Evidence: billing provider ports and runtime proof inputs.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is provider-backed: in-memory provider proof reports ready, Lago proof checks /health or SDK public-key path, and control/readiness route proofs classify adapter availability separately from catalog seed readiness. Evidence: proof:billing-provider and proof:lago-billing-provider.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:billing-provider validates in-memory provider account/payment/refund flow; proof:lago-billing-provider validates live HTTP-backed Lago customer/plan/subscription/cancel/invoice flow. Unit proof: lago-billing-provider.test.ts. Proof level: 4 provider substrate validation plus level 3 behavior/provider seam validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose account/customer state, plan/subscription/invoice/payment method/dunning status tables, provider readiness states, payment capture/refund outcomes, idempotent submission behavior, tenant read permissions, and operator-only provider actions.

Evidence: metering usecase and runtime proofs.
semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is METER_CATALOG with meterKey, entitlementKey, displayName, unit, plus meter_events rows containing organisation, meter, quantity, idempotency key, subject/source/metadata, and occurred time. Evidence: metering usecase and repository proof.
semanticCompleteness.permissions: Ingestion is global/operator-only via platform.metering.write; tenant self-ingestion is not exposed. Tenant reads require tenant.metering.read; operator reads require platform.metering.read. Evidence: routes.ts and metering-quota route proof.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are POST /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/meter-events, GET /api/org/usage, and GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/usage with UsageResponse meter rows. Evidence: routes.ts and contracts-admin metering schemas.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Validation rejects invalid tenant ids, invalid request bodies, unknown meter keys, non-finite quantity, and negative quantity unless metadata.adjustment is true; entitlement denial happens before record insertion. Evidence: metering usecase and live metering proof.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Errors include VALIDATION_ERROR, NOT_FOUND unknown meter, ForbiddenError notEntitled, tenant-context NO_TENANT for tenant reads, 201 recorded, and 200 deduplicated replay. Evidence: routes.ts and metering-quota route proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Individual meter events are the usage data and are not audit events; quota changes that influence enforcement are audited separately. Evidence: metering usecase comments and quota usecase.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is live Postgres-backed with honest skip when Postgres is unavailable; proof validates RLS tenant isolation, operator rls_bypass read, windowed aggregation, and no secret columns. Evidence: apps/platform-api/scripts/metering-runtime-proof.ts.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:metering validates live Postgres idempotency, entitlement gate, aggregation, RLS isolation, and no secret columns; proof:metering-quota-routes validates live route handlers and access-control metadata. Proof level: 4 live substrate/route validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose read-only usage tables, meter labels/units/windows, tenant/operator views, deduplicated ingestion status only on operator surfaces, entitlement-denied and no-tenant states, no tenant self-ingestion controls, and no secret-bearing fields.

semanticCompleteness.stateModel: State model is QuotaRecord with quotaKey, entitlementKey, meterKey, limit, window, action deny, updatedAt/updatedBy plus computed state within|exceeded|no_entitlement|no_quota, usage, allowed, and projectedUsage for delta checks. semanticCompleteness.permissions: Tenant quota reads use tenant.metering.read; operator reads use platform.quotas.read; operator set uses platform.quotas.write; enforcement runs after pipeline permission and entitlement checks. Evidence: routes.ts and route proof.
semanticCompleteness.contracts: Contracts are GET /api/org/quotas, GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/quotas, PATCH /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/quotas, plus internal assertQuota/assertQuotaWithDelta for guarded operations. Evidence: routes.ts and quota usecase.
semanticCompleteness.validation: Routes validate tenant UUIDs and SetQuotaRequestSchema; usecase treats absent quota as opt-in allowed, validates entitlement before usage comparison, and evaluates deltas for operations that add usage. Evidence: routes.ts and quota tests.
semanticCompleteness.errorModel: Denials are explicit: no_entitlement yields notEntitled ForbiddenError, exceeded yields quotaExceeded ForbiddenError with safe quota/usage/limit/window details, invalid route bodies return VALIDATION_ERROR, and no_quota remains allowed. Evidence: quota usecase and runtime proof.
semanticCompleteness.auditModel: Operator setQuota emits quota.set audit before repository upsert with entitlementKey, meterKey, limit, window, and action; audit failure prevents the quota change. Evidence: quota usecase and unit tests.
semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is live Postgres-backed with honest skip when unavailable; runtime proof validates set/evaluate/assert across entitlement grant/revoke and metered usage. Evidence: quota-enforcement-runtime-proof.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Runtime proofs: proof:quota-enforcement validates live entitlement-before-quota ordering, quota exceeded state, assertQuota typed denial, revoke-denial, and no_quota allow; proof:metering-quota-routes validates live route handlers. Proof level: 4 live substrate/route validation.
semanticCompleteness.uiSemanticDefinition: V2 UI must expose quota list with key, entitlement, meter, limit, window, usage, allowed, state, updated metadata, operator set form, tenant read-only view, no_quota/no_entitlement/exceeded states, validation errors, and audit-backed save semantics.

retention.proof: Log retention is governed by the observability substrate and bounded query window semantics: tenant readiness probes query only the last five minutes, admin search clamps limits to 1..1000, and durable tenant audit/history records that need platform retention are covered by the retention runtime proof rather than hidden in Loki.

backup.proof: Operational log search is a read-only Loki-backed surface and does not create additional repository state; durable observability configuration and tenant audit/history records are covered by platform backup scripts, while Loki reachability and query behavior are proven by the tenant-observability runtime proof.

restore.proof: Restore semantics for this capability are provider-readiness based: after restoring platform state, log search reuses the Loki adapter and bounded query contract; durable tenant audit/history records are restored through the platform PITR and backup-restore proofs.

export.proof: Tenant export covers durable audit/history records that are the governed tenant record; raw Loki lines remain operational logs exposed only through permissioned, bounded search. The data-portability proof exports audit/history, while the log search proof constrains direct operational log retrieval by filters, direction, and limit.

legal hold.proof: Legal hold applies to durable tenant audit/history records through the platform legal-hold controls; log search itself is read-only operational retrieval and cannot delete or mutate Loki data. Originating capability mutations remain responsible for legal-hold-aware durable records.

DSR.proof: DSR/data-portability uses the tenant export path for governed audit/history data. Raw operational Loki entries are not exported wholesale; they are accessible only through authorized admin log search with normalized criteria and tenant/request/actor JSON filters.

lineage.proof: Log lineage is explicit: runtime code emits structured platform logs with requestId/traceId/actorId/tenantId fields, Loki adapter stores low-cardinality service/level labels and high-cardinality ids as JSON fields, search normalizes limit/direction, parses Loki responses, and returns labels plus parsed fields.

tenant isolation.proof: Tenant isolation is enforced through permissioned route access and tenant-scoped JSON-field filtering: admin log search requires platform.logs.read, tenant readiness queries include organisationId as a JSON filter, and the high-cardinality guard proves tenant/request/actor ids are not promoted to shared Loki labels.

classification.proof: History entries are classified by source and safe type: audit action, platform event type, notification category, incident status, or meter key. The port exposes only id, source, type, title, occurredAt, and actorId, with source filtering and pagination.

retention.proof: History is a read-only projection over retained source tables rather than a copied store. Retention is owned by audit/events/notification/incident/meter source records and the platform retention proof; history queries do not create or delete retained rows.

backup.proof: Because history introduces no new store, backup coverage is the backup coverage of its Postgres source tables. The local backup and restore scripts cover database-backed audit/events/notification/incident/meter rows, and history proof validates the projection from those live rows.

restore.proof: Restore coverage follows the source Postgres tables: after PITR or backup restore, history is re-derived by SELECT-only UNION queries from restored audit/events/notification/incident/meter rows without replaying or duplicating projection state.

export.proof: Tenant export includes the safe history projection as audit/history.json. The data portability proof builds an encrypted archive containing audit/history.json and verifies manifest order, digests, tenant binding, tamper rejection, and durable import of the history group.

legal hold.proof: Legal hold applies to the durable source rows, not to a separate history store. History is read-only and cannot bypass holds; legal-hold runtime proofs cover held source retention/deletion behavior while history proof verifies queries leave source rows unchanged.

DSR.proof: DSR/data-portability uses the tenant export path to include audit/history.json, generated from the history read model. The projection deliberately excludes raw metadata, payload, token, credential, and secret fields before export.

lineage.proof: Lineage is explicit in the UNION branches: audit_events, platform_events, notification_log, incidents, and meter_events each project only safe summary columns with an organisation predicate; routes pass either the session tenant or validated operator-selected tenant to getHistory.

semanticCompleteness.readinessModel: Readiness is FULL only when all required stage groups pass and ladder evidence is current, ordered, single-SHA, and attested; otherwise stage/ladder readiness is degraded or failed. Evidence: verify-ladder and aggregate-confidence tests.
semanticCompleteness.proof: Proofs: scripts/evidence/tests/verify-ladder.test.mjs, scripts/tests/tests/aggregate-confidence.test.mjs, stage-exit tests, and make all stage evidence. Proof level: 3 state-machine validation.
